Product: Lactomaxin 100 mg/syringe intramammary infusion (lactomaxin); strength 100 mg/syringe; route intramammary.

Antimicrobial Classification: Reserve; stewardship warning and prudent-use statement included.

Patient Information Leaflet for dossier VET-NEW-INNOV-AMR-LACT-0107.
Product: Lactomaxin 100 mg/syringe intramammary infusion (lactomaxin); strength 100 mg/syringe; route intramammary.
Applicant: Horizon Pharma Distributors Ltd (Rwanda); Manufacturer: NovaCare Healthcare Ltd (China).
PIL Section: What this medicine is and what it is used for.
PIL Route and Form: Route=intramammary; Dosage form=intramammary infusion; Strength=100 mg/syringe.
PIL Instructions: Follow the prescribed dose schedule. Do not exceed recommended dose.
PIL Warnings: Do not use if you are allergic to the active ingredient or related medicines. Seek immediate medical care for rash, breathing difficulty, or severe diarrhoea. Use exactly as prescribed; complete the full treatment course where applicable. For veterinary use in dairy cattle only.
PIL Storage: Store below 30C, protect from moisture/light, keep out of reach of children.
PIL Adverse Effects: Common effects include gastrointestinal upset and headache; serious effects require urgent review.
Antimicrobial Classification: Reserve; stewardship warning and prudent-use statement included.

SmPC for dossier VET-NEW-INNOV-AMR-LACT-0107.
Product: Lactomaxin 100 mg/syringe intramammary infusion (lactomaxin); strength 100 mg/syringe; route intramammary.
Applicant: Horizon Pharma Distributors Ltd (Rwanda); Manufacturer: NovaCare Healthcare Ltd (China).
SmPC 4.1 Therapeutic indications: As approved in the application form.
SmPC 4.2 Posology and method of administration: Dose by age/weight and clinical condition as approved.
SmPC 4.3 Contraindications: Known hypersensitivity to active ingredient or excipients, Severe allergy to related therapeutic class where applicable.
SmPC 4.4 Special warnings and precautions: monitor organ function and hypersensitivity risks.
SmPC 4.8 Undesirable effects: nausea, diarrhoea, rash, dizziness; serious adverse reactions listed.
SmPC 6.4 Special precautions for storage: store according to label and stability commitments.
Antimicrobial Classification: Reserve; stewardship warning and prudent-use statement included.
